task.title: Add merge-governance examples for GitHub branch protection and merge queues that consume repo-contract-kit readiness signals.

context.problem_statement: Docs contract should be a merge prerequisite where desired, but hosted merge governance must stay an optional adapter over the local repo-contract-kit readiness contract.
background: AGW-052 is complete with branch-readiness / make agent-branch-readiness.
AGW-053 is docs/examples work for owner-operated GitHub protected branch, status check, and merge queue governance.
repo-contract-kit must not mutate GitHub state or require credentials.
non_goals: No PR comments, labels, approvals, branch-protection edits, or merge queue mutation.
No change to branch-readiness behavior unless a docs example exposes a scoped bug.

previous_task_state.notes: AGW-053 is the next selected open item after AGW-052 was closed as shipped and revalidated. repo-contract-kit is clean at 76e2129 / 0.6.1 after the target-migration JSON update fix.

protected_files: GitHub workflows or hosted governance automation unless explicitly docs-only
credentials, tokens, branch protection settings, PR mutation, merge queue mutation

acceptance_criteria: Docs include owner-operated branch-readiness gate examples.
Docs map local readiness evidence to GitHub protected branch/status check concepts without claiming repo-contract-kit publishes hosted checks.
Docs include a merge queue handoff example that preserves owner/platform authority.
Examples distinguish required and advisory checks via checks-json.
Docs state no GitHub mutation, credentials, PR comments, or merge queue operations are performed by repo-contract-kit.

commands: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 make test if tests or templates change
